methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $500M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.0B - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Rain AI is a Series A chip-design company valued at $500M post-money following $146M raised. The valuation assessment draws on a universe of 8 private peers in chips and semiconductors (including Etched, d-Matrix, and Substrate at $5.0B, $2.0B, and $1.0B respectively) and 3 public semiconductor benchmarks (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

Two methods yield quantifiable outputs. Stage-progression applies a standard 2.0x step-up multiplier from the $500M Series A post-money valuation, implying $1.0B. Peer-median triangulation across three same-sector, same-stage comparables yields $2.0B, reflecting a wide range of $1.0B to $5.0B across the sample. Public-comp anchoring using the sector's 20.6x median price-to-sales multiple cannot be applied due to absence of disclosed target revenue, typical for private companies.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $850M to $2.3B, with a median of $1.5B. This median sits between the stage-progression and peer-median outputs, appropriately weighting each method. Key uncertainties include the appropriate peer set (the $5.0B Etched valuation materially influences the median) and Rain AI's progress relative to comparables on technical milestones and customer adoption. The current $500M valuation falls below this range, suggesting meaningful step-up potential conditional on execution.
